chunk_summary: This section outlines stepwise plotting of BMI using IAP growth charts for children aged 5–18, emphasizing error prevention in OPD settings and severity classification for obesity management.
learning_objectives: Understand the stepwise process for plotting BMI on IAP growth charts for ages 5–18.
Learn methods to prevent common OPD errors in anthropometric measurements and BMI classification.
Apply severity classification for obesity using IAP percent-of-cutoff guidelines for escalation planning.
key_facts: BMI is calculated as weight (kg) divided by height (m) squared.
IAP BMI charts are sex-specific and applicable for ages 5–18.
Adult-equivalent BMI cutoffs: below 23, between 23–27, and at/above 27.
Severe obesity staging: Class 2 (120–140% of age-specific cutoff) and Class 3 (≥140%).
Common OPD errors include age miscalculation and incorrect height/weight measurements.
reference_quote: Never apply adult cutoffs (25/30) to children in Indian practice because this delays recognition of risk.
document_key_topics: IAP BMI chart plotting for ages 5–18
